name: Current Royals
description: Windsors, weddings, jubilees, and corgis — modern monarchy from Elizabeth II to William and Kate.

questions.q: In what year was Queen Elizabeth II's coronation?

source.notes: Crowned June 2, 1953. Acceded to the throne February 6, 1952 (after George VI's death).

questions.q: In what year did Princess Elizabeth marry Prince Philip?
What dog breed was Queen Elizabeth II famously associated with?

source.notes: Specifically Pembroke Welsh Corgis. She owned more than 30 over her lifetime.

questions.q: How many children did Queen Elizabeth II have?
In what country was Princess Elizabeth when she learned she had become Queen?

source.notes: On February 6, 1952 at Treetops Hotel during a Commonwealth tour.

questions.q: On what day of the week did Queen Elizabeth II traditionally hold her weekly audience with the UK Prime Minister?
Which film franchise did Queen Elizabeth II spoof in the 2012 London Olympics opening ceremony?

source.notes: She appeared with Daniel Craig as 007.

questions.q: Which jubilee did Queen Elizabeth II celebrate in 2012, marking 60 years on the throne?

source.notes: She also reached the Platinum Jubilee (70 years) in 2022 — a first in British history.

questions.q: In what year was King Charles III (then Prince Charles) born?

source.notes: Born November 14, 1948. Acceded to the throne in 2022.

questions.q: On what date did Prince William marry Catherine 'Kate' Middleton at Westminster Abbey?

source.notes: Watched globally by an estimated 2 billion people.

questions.q: In what year was the Princess of Wales (Catherine 'Kate' Middleton) born?
What is the name of Prince William and Kate Middleton's eldest child?

source.notes: Prince George of Wales, born July 22, 2013.

questions.q: What is the name of Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's first child, born in May 2019?
Crown Princess Mary of Denmark (now Queen Mary) was born in which country?

source.notes: Born Mary Donaldson in Hobart, Tasmania, Australia.

questions.q: Which royal won an Olympic silver medal in equestrian eventing at London 2012?

source.notes: Zara won silver as part of the Great Britain eventing team.

questions.q: Which earlier royal had competed in the 1976 Olympics in equestrian events?

source.notes: Anne competed in eventing at Montreal 1976. The first royal Olympian.

questions.q: What sport did Zara Phillips' husband, Mike Tindall, play professionally?

source.notes: Tindall captained England's rugby team and won the 2003 Rugby World Cup.

questions.q: Camilla Parker Bowles became which title upon marrying Charles in 2005?

source.notes: She declined to use 'Princess of Wales' in deference to Diana. Now Queen Camilla.

questions.q: How many sovereign countries does the British monarch serve as head of state, including the UK?

source.notes: 15 Commonwealth realms in addition to the UK, including Canada, Australia, NZ, Jamaica, Papua New Guinea, etc. Original answer reflected pre-Barbados-republic 16; current count is 15. Note: Barbados removed the monarch in 2021.

questions.q: Title-wise, who is the current 'Prince of Wales' as of 2022 onwards?

source.notes: Charles held the title 1958–2022; passed to William when Charles became king.
